behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is template-lint logic on prompt text, not a cross-module runtime failure. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: Mocks do not hide the core failure because the failure is local string/AST rule evaluation. LLM-DEP: No model output dependency. MULTI-STEP: No agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies needing runtime module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run lint rule function on prompt template fixtures: one with copied phase instructions (must fail with precise violation), one clean template (must pass), and one near-miss paraphrase to verify rule specificity.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture assembly), not a single function only. REAL-INFRA: No real network/subprocess needed; local harness wiring is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: Unit-only checks can miss leakage introduced during assembly. LLM-DEP: No dependence on model judgment. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic sequence. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. Slot A: "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures)". Slot B: boundary is harness assembly pipeline. Slot C: unit lint alone misses leakage injected post-template during interpolation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real prompt-construction path with production-like fixtures/interpolation, capture final prompt string, and assert forbidden phase-instruction patterns are absent in assembled output.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is incorrect source-of-truth handoff (file-loaded skill context vs hard-coded prompt content) across harness modules. REAL-INFRA: Local filesystem read path is enough; no real network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: Pure unit tests of prompt builder can miss that invocation path bypasses skill files. LLM-DEP: Behavior here is provenance/loading, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: Not a chained agentic pipeline. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. Slot A: "loads skill context from plugin/skill files". Slot B: boundary is invocation loader -> prompt assembly. Slot C: unit tests cannot prove runtime invocation did not use hard-coded rules.
behaviors.test_description: Run invocation through real harness with skill file loader enabled; assert resolved context includes plugin/skill file content and that test prompt text itself contains no hard-coded phase rules.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure arises when harness variant-selection and file-loading interact while keeping prompt text constant. REAL-INFRA: Needs local file variants but not OS-level primitives beyond normal reads; no external network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: Unit tests can miss accidental prompt mutation or fixed-skill path coupling. LLM-DEP: This behavior checks harness capability, not output quality. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic chain required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. Slot A: "same user-style prompt ... degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text". Slot B: boundary is variant fixture selection + invocation pipeline. Slot C: lower-level tests miss regressions where harness silently rewrites prompt per variant.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness over baseline/degraded/missing SKILL.md fixtures and assert identical user prompt payload bytes are sent in each run while context source changes by fixture.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is not just wiring; it depends on whether model behavior changes when skill guidance is degraded. REAL-INFRA: Usually real model API call path is involved. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbed model would return fixed output and could falsely pass, hiding regression in skill-conditioned behavior. LLM-DEP: Yes, correctness depends on real LLM decisions under changed context. MULTI-STEP: Single agentic response is sufficient; no mandatory multi-step pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because text requires "behavioral test fails" after skill degradation under same prompt, which directly tests model-conditioned behavior quality. Slot A: "When ... SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails". Slot B: boundary is real model response conditioned on external skill context. Slot C: integration with deterministic stub cannot detect loss of behavior sensitivity to skill content.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke test twice (baseline skill vs degraded/missing section) with identical user-style prompt; assert structural behavior passes baseline and fails degraded variant with recorded response artifacts.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The bug appears in sequencing across model-call wrapper, artifact writer, and parser modules. REAL-INFRA: Local filesystem artifact write is enough; no real network/subprocess needed to validate ordering. MOCK-HIDE: Unit-testing parser alone misses pre-parse capture ordering regressions. LLM-DEP: Output content quality is irrelevant; can use deterministic fake response body. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic sequence. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no. Slot A: "captured ... before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs". Slot B: boundary is call wrapper -> artifact sink -> parser order. Slot C: lower-level tests can pass while integration order is wrong.
behaviors.test_description: Execute harness path with controlled model response payload, then assert artifact file contains raw bytes and timestamp/order proves write occurs before parser invocation (or before parse errors are raised).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is assertion-rule logic (schema/path/role placement validation), primarily local and deterministic. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: Mocks do not hide failure because rule evaluation can be tested directly on fixtures. LLM-DEP: No dependency on live model quality for this behavior statement itself. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior focuses on assertion semantics, not module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion module with crafted outputs: keyword-present-but-structurally-wrong cases must fail; structurally-correct cases must pass; include role-field misplacement negatives.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Requires real generated outputs where keywords may appear but structure regresses. REAL-INFRA: Live model execution implies real API/runtime path. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked fixed outputs can be curated to pass and miss naturally occurring structural drift. LLM-DEP: Yes, correctness depends on what the real model returns. MULTI-STEP: Single response evaluation; not necessarily multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration due to explicit phrase "Under live model execution," which disqualifies stub-only validation for real failure detection. Slot A: "Under live model execution" and "keyword-only check would miss". Slot B: boundary is real model output variability vs structural validator. Slot C: integration with canned fixtures cannot prove detection of real-world model regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live-model calls on fixed user-style prompt(s); evaluate both keyword-only and structural assertions, and require at least one case where keyword check passes but structural check fails to demonstrate regression catch capability.
